data.title: Uniswap Foundation Reports Q1 Financial Summary for 2025
data.description:
According to Odaily, the Uniswap Foundation has released its financial summary for the first quarter ending March 31, 2025. The report highlights that following the "Uniswap Unleashed" governance proposal, the foundation's total assets amount to $95 million. This includes $53.4 million in cash and stablecoins, 15.8 million UNI tokens, 257 ETH, and an external collateral loan arrangement of 5 million UNI.

The foundation anticipates that its operational funds will last until January 2027. It has allocated a total of $115.1 million for grants and incentives, with $99.8 million committed for 2025-2026 and $15.3 million already pledged but not yet disbursed. Additionally, $33.3 million is designated for operational and employee incentive expenses.

During the first quarter, the foundation committed to new grants totaling $12.4 million, with $2.1 million actually disbursed. Nearly 80% of these grants are intended for long-term projects spanning from 2026 to 2029. The foundation also confirmed that its operational expenses for Q1 amounted to $1.9 million. The primary source of income for the foundation during this period was $140 million raised through governance donations.

data.title: Stablecoin project Plasma receives strategic investment from Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The stablecoin project Plasma officially announced today that it has received strategic investment from Peter Thiel's Founders Fund. This investment will help Plasma expand its team, expand its ecosystem and accelerate its adoption in Latin America, the Middle East and other regions.

data.title: Uniswap Foundation: $115 million allocated for the entire year of 2024
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The Uniswap Foundation released a Q1 financial summary as of March 31, 2025, showing that after passing the "Uniswap Unleashed" governance proposal, the foundation held a total of $95 million in assets, including $53.4 million in cash and stablecoins, 15.8 million UNI tokens, 257 ETH, and an external mortgage arrangement of 5 million UNI.
The Foundation expects operating funds to support until January 2027, of which a total of $115.1 million will be used for grants and incentives (99.8 million US dollars pledged in 2025-2026, and $15.3 million has been pledged to be issued), and operating and employee incentive expenses are $33.3 million. During Q1, the Foundation pledged new grants of $12.4 million and actually issued $2.1 million, nearly 80% of which were long-term grants from 2026 to 2029. The foundation also confirmed Q1 operating expenses of $1.9 million and raised $140 million in donations through governance as its primary source of revenue.

data.title: Swedish Medical Tech Firm Acquires Bitcoin for Treasury Reserves
data.description: According to PANews, Swedish medical technology company H100 Group has announced the acquisition of 4.39 bitcoins for 5 million Norwegian kroner, approximately $475,000. This move makes H100 Group the first publicly listed company in Sweden to include Bitcoin in its treasury reserves.

data.title: Bitcoin Price Surge Anticipated by Investor James Wynn
data.description: According to Odaily, prominent investor James Wynn shared on the X platform that he has updated his small Bitcoin long positions, earning approximately $111,000 to $111,100 in profits through position reduction. Wynn expressed optimism about Bitcoin's imminent breakthrough, predicting a price target of $115,000 to $118,000 by next weekend, though he noted this could occur within hours. He cautioned that once Bitcoin reaches a peak of around $118,000 to $122,000, the market might cool down and experience some lateral movement, leading to potentially interesting developments. Wynn also mentioned the term 'altcoin season' in his post.

data.title: James Wynn: If Bitcoin reaches a peak of $118,000-122,000, the market may see a cooling or sideways movement
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: James Wynn, a whale, published a post on the X platform to disclose that he has updated his small long position in Bitcoin, and said that he has made a profit of about $110,000 to $111,000 by reducing his position. James Wynn added that Bitcoin is eager to break through, and his personal price target is still to break through $115,000 to $118,000 next weekend, but this price may even happen within a few hours. However, once Bitcoin reaches a peak of around $118,000 to $122,000, the market may cool down and experience some sideways fluctuations, and then things will become very interesting. In addition, James Wynn also wrote "copycat season".

data.title: Believe suspends Launchcoin automatic listing and switches to manual review
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Web3 launch platform Believe announced that it has adjusted the listing process. Due to the recent emergence of a large number of tokens without actual project support, the automatic listing function released through the @launchcoin account has been temporarily suspended. Project parties can still submit applications through the official website, and all projects will be released after manual review.
At the same time, the original "Featured" label will be renamed "Verified", which means that the project has had basic exchanges with the team and shows real construction intentions. Although the official does not guarantee its legitimacy, it will do its best to review the submitted verification requests. The new mechanism will be gradually launched on the same day.

data.title: Michigan lawmakers file 4 crypto bills on retiree funds, CBDCs, mining
data.description:
Michigan lawmakers have introduced four crypto-related bills covering crypto mining, central bank digital currencies (CBDCs) and crypto in state retirement funds. Republican state Representative Bill Schuette introduced House Bill 4510 on May 21, which would amend Michigan’s Public Employee Retirement System Investment Act to allow the state treasurer, currently Rachael Eubanks, to invest in cryptocurrencies that have averaged a market cap above $250 million over the last calendar year. Bitcoin (BTC) and Ether (ETH) are the only cryptocurrencies that currently meet that threshold. The bill adds that any cryptocurrencies must be held in the form of an exchange-traded product issued by a registered investment company
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/michigan-lawmakers-introduce-four-crypto-bills?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Supreme Court Ruling on Presidential Authority in Federal Appointments
data.description: According to PANews, the Supreme Court has ruled in the case of 'Trump v. Wilcox,' allowing U.S. President Donald Trump to dismiss two Democratic officials from the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) and the Merit Systems Protection Board (MSPB) without legal grounds for termination. The court justified this decision as part of the exercise of presidential executive power. However, the ruling explicitly does not apply to the Federal Reserve, which is described as a 'uniquely structured quasi-private entity,' thereby safeguarding Federal Reserve Chairman Powell from dismissal.

data.title: Major Banks Consider Stablecoin Launch Amid Crypto Competition
data.description: According to Foresight News, major commercial banks such as JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo are in discussions to jointly issue a stablecoin. This move is aimed at addressing the increasing competition from the cryptocurrency industry. The negotiations involve Early Warning Services, the operator of the Zelle payment system, and the real-time payment network Clearing House.

data.title: Immutable Reduces Workforce by One-Third Amid Financial Challenges
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Australian blockchain gaming company Immutable, backed by Temasek, has reduced its workforce by one-third over the past nine months. The number of employees has decreased from 224 in 2023 to approximately 150.

The company confirmed that some roles in its business, engineering, product, and marketing teams are now supported by AI, particularly in design and content creation.

Despite incurring a $50 million loss in 2023 due to rising employee costs and declining crypto assets, Immutable reported a 55% revenue increase in 2024, reaching $67 million. The company anticipates achieving cash flow breakeven by the end of 2025.

data.title: Glassnode: Bitcoin hits new all-time high, but profit-taking volume plummets to $1 billion
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The on-chain data platform Glassnode reported that although Bitcoin hit an all-time high in May 2025, the overall profit-taking amount in the market has dropped significantly to only $1 billion, less than half of the $2.1 billion at the peak in December 2024.
The data also showed that the current market is dominated by short-term traders, with 76.9% of BTC being "recent holdings" within 1 month, while the proportion of "long-term holdings" held for more than 6 months has dropped to 13.4%. Long-term holders have significantly reduced their transactions, reflecting their bullish attitude towards the market outlook.
The analysis pointed out that as the price of BTC rose to $111,500, some investors began to turn their attention to Altcoins with higher potential returns, which may trigger a change in the market's focus.
